mission.title: prometheus: Allow relative URL in web.external-url
mission.description: Allow relative URL in web.external-url. Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current prometheus deployment
steps.description:
Verify your prometheus version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
helm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working prometheus installation.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
I'm serving Prometheus behind a reverse-proxy under a `/prometheus` path, so in 0.16, I'd set `web.external-url` to `/prometheus`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow relative URL in web.external-url
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
ERRO[0000] invalid Prometheus URL: /prometheus           source=main.go:65
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow relative URL in web.external-url" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/pull/1791

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
